accepted.reason: Anisotropic diffusion stencils are high-signal numeric kernels with nested grid loops.
Particle position/velocity integration is a reusable simulation workload with straightforward tight loops.
Objective aggregation and action scoring loops are practical optimization kernels for model training.
Oscillator-bank synthesis provides numeric inner loops common in DSP/audio workloads.
Bond-distance geometry computations are clean vector-math kernels suitable for Cython conversion tasks.

skipped.reason: Protocol decoding appears format/integration-specific and less reusable as a generic optimization exemplar.
Cython runtime feature tests are not standalone algorithmic optimization targets.
Callback/demo wrapper code is glue-heavy and low signal for general Cython speedup training.
Compiler regression test content is not appropriate for reusable GRPO optimization data.
I/O polling wrappers are integration/system-call oriented rather than compute-kernel optimization examples.
